titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Angela Mackail Thirkell Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4119987.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 1, 2022 at 03:59
Added Series V: Gift of JoAnn Weaver and computer media

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection contains: holograph manuscript drafts of Thirkell's novels Private Enterprise and What Did it Mean?; correspondence between Thirkell and James Tinkham Babb, Barbara Waite Davidson, Gordon and Mary Haight, and others; third-party correspondence by Lance Thirkell, Laura Roberts Collins, C. A. Lejuene, and others; clippings and printed ephemera relating to Thirkell and her work; and computer media.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Type of accession (gift or purchase) and date of acquisition is noted in the box-and-folder list. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Angela Mackail Thirkell Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Angela Mackail Thirkell Collection. General Collection. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 5 and 6 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from previously existing cards in the Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained. If the collection also contains unlisted or unsorted material, it has been noted in the box-and-folder list. This finding aid will be updated periodically to account for new materials that may be added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been added since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to Angela Mackail Thirkell, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection. Materials have been arranged into four series: Gift of Angela Mackail Thirkell, Gift of Gordon Haight, Gift of Margaret Bradley Babb, and Gift of the Angela Thirkell Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in pencil in eleven notebooks

did.unittitle: Thirkell, Angela Mackail. TLS to Gordon S. Haight. London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from Private Enterprise , autograph manuscript

did.unittitle: What Did It Mean? , autograph manuscript
Babb, James Tinkham. 2 TLS (carbons) to Angela Thirkell, New Haven
Haight, Gordon Sherman. TL (carbon) to George Sherburn, New Haven

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On the same leaf: Haight, Gordon Sherman. TL (carbon) to Willard Thorpe. New Haven, 1948 Nov 8

did.unittitle: Haight, Gordon Sherman. 33 TL (carbons) to Angela Thirkell
Kunkel, Beverly W. ALS to Gordon S. Haight, Easton, PA
Sherburn, George. TLS to Gordon S. Haight, Cambridge, Mass.
Thirkell, Angela. 41 ALS, 7 APCS, 5 ACS, 34 TLS (some with manuscript notes), and 2 TL to Gordon and Mary Haight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With clippings, business and change of address cards, etc. (6 items)

did.unittitle: [Thirkell], Lance. ALS to Gordon S. Haight, London
Whitney, J. B. TLS (with manuscript note) to Gordon S. Haight. Cambridge, Mass.
3 printed invitations and 1 business card (some with manuscript notes)
Thirkell, Angela. TLS to Mrs. Babb, London

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Angela Thirkell Society, 2008 and 2009.

did.unittitle: Correspondence between Angela Thirkell and Barbara Waite Davidson
Letters from Angela Thirkell to Mary Lois Mamer
Copies of letters from Angela Thirkell to others
Correspondence between Lance Thirkell and Laura Roberts Collins
Letters from Lance Thirkell to Phyllis Croghan
Correspondence between Lance Thirkell and Barbara Waite Davidson
Correspondence between C. A. Lejeune and Barbara Waite Davidson
Transcript of telephone conversation between Angela Thirkell and Colin Macinnes, BBC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 12 letters and one postcard to J. T. Stewart and Mr. Bonip from Angela Thirkell.

did.unittitle: Correspondence between Angela Thirkell and J. T. Stewart
Letter from Angela Thirkell to Mr. Bonip
